message:
fix: cache SHOW DATABASES results to prevent thundering herd (#2180)

Multiple concurrent callers (status-line, daemon, bd list, health checks)
all fire SHOW DATABASES queries simultaneously, each spawning a ~200MB
dolt sql subprocess. In a 6-agent container this pins the server at 99% CPU.

Add a 30-second TTL cache with single-flight deduplication to
ListDatabases for remote servers. Concurrent callers share one in-flight
query instead of each spawning their own subprocess.

Also redirect daemon.DoltServerManager.listDatabases to use the cached
doltserver.ListDatabases, eliminating its duplicate SHOW DATABASES
subprocess spawning.

Cache is invalidated after InitRig (CREATE DATABASE) and database
removal (DROP DATABASE + directory cleanup).

Fix-merged from PR #2234: also fixed listDatabasesRemote to use
IsSystemDatabase() instead of hardcoded check (was missing dolt_cluster
filtering, which the daemon old code had via IsSystemDatabase).

Closes #2180

Co-Authored-By: Chris Deal <cdeal@mines.edu>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
